name: Cap Angela (Ras Ben Sakka)
description: Northernmost point of Africa and the entire African continent. Rocky headland with dramatic Mediterranean views, popular wild bivouac spot among Tunisian campers.

name: Cap Serrat
description: Hidden gem on the north coast with pristine sandy coves, turquoise water and cork oak backdrop. One of Tunisia's most beautiful wild beaches for camping.

name: Kef Abed
description: Remote rocky massif near Bizerte with spectacular night skies and calm ambiance. A classic wild camping destination for Tunisian outdoor groups.

name: Lac Ichkeul – Rive Nord
description: UNESCO World Heritage freshwater lake surrounded by marshes and Jebel Ichkeul. Outstanding birdwatching zone; wild camping on the northern bank away from the visitor center.

name: Jebel Ichkeul – Versant Sud
description: Forested southern slopes of Jebel Ichkeul rising above the lake. Shaded bivouac areas among maquis and olive trees with panoramic lake views.

name: Plage de Sounine
description: Wild sandy beach west of Bizerte city, accessible via dirt track. Open dune camping with calm sea and few visitors outside summer.

name: Plage de Ras Engela
description: Isolated headland beach near Sejnane with dramatic rocky outcrops and clear Mediterranean water. Minimal infrastructure, ideal for wild camping.

name: Sidi El Barrak Dam Surroundings
description: Large artificial lake within Cap Négro–Chitana National Park zone. Forested banks with cork oaks provide excellent sheltered camping amid nature.

name: El Mgasseb
description: One of the most pristine wild spots in northern Tunisia. Deep cork oak forest with a river valley, considered a virgin camping location by local outdoor communities.

name: Oued Mejerda – Rive de Testour
description: Riverside camping along the Mejerda River near the historic town of Testour. Riparian forest with lush vegetation and calm waters.

name: Forêt de Bellif
description: Dense mixed forest within the Cap Négro protected area. Cork oaks, holm oaks and rare peat bog habitat. Remote and barely visited.

name: Jebel Tebaba
description: Isolated mountain in Béja governorate with open limestone ridges and panoramic views over northern Tunisia's valleys. Low foot traffic.

name: Ain Draham – Forêt de Kroumirie
description: Vast cork oak and holm oak forest in the Kroumirie Mountains. Coolest and most humid region in Tunisia, with excellent forested clearings for wild camping.

name: Bni Mtir Dam
description: Scenic dam surrounded completely by forested hills near Ain Draham. Campers set up tents on the shore with views of the glassy reservoir and rolling green hills.

name: El Feija National Park – Zone Périphérique
description: Peripheral zones of El Feija NP feature dense oak forest with Barbary deer. Camping is practiced in buffer areas; check with the local forestry office.

name: Oued Zeen – Vallée Basse
description: Valley of the Zeen River within Oued Zeen National Park. Wooded riverbanks with oak and wild olive trees. Hiking and camping are common activities here.

name: Hammam Bourguiba Forest
description: Cork oak forest near the thermal springs of Hammam Bourguiba. Cooler temperatures and shade make this a popular wild camping spot for northern Tunisian campers.

name: Jebel Bir – Kroumirie
description: Remote summit in the Kroumirie range with forest approaches. Panoramic views over Algeria and northern Tunisia. Rarely visited by outsiders.

name: Forêt de Mhimech
description: Mixed cork oak and pine forest south of Ain Draham with river streams and rocky outcrops. Natural clearings suitable for tents.

name: Lac de Ghezala
description: Small natural lake in the northwest forests, near the Algerian border. Surrounded by cork oaks and maquis shrubland; tranquil wild camping.

name: Jebel Sidi Abdelaziz
description: Forested mountain north of Jendouba city with a marabout shrine at the summit. Natural forest camps on the slopes among pines.

name: Ain Soltane Forest
description: Protected forest near El Feija; cork oaks with clear streams running through. A lesser-known spot near the Algerian frontier.

name: Tabarka – Plage de la Galite (Offshore)
description: Wild beaches east of Tabarka port with rocky pine-backed coves. Popular with local campers on weekends in summer.

name: Plage Zouaraa
description: Undeveloped beach near Zouaraa with fine sand and calm sea. Pine forest directly backs the beach for shaded camping.

name: Jebel Zaghouan – Versant Nord
description: Northern face of Jebel Zaghouan, Tunisia's most iconic mountain peak at 1295 m. Rocky approaches with cedar and pine; wild camping below the crags.

name: Jebel Zaghouan – Source des Nymphes
description: Natural springs and Roman temple ruins at the foot of Zaghouan. Wooded area around the ancient water source, used for overnight stays by hikers.

name: Jebel Ressas
description: Rocky limestone massif southeast of Tunis with Aleppo pine slopes. Popular for day hikes and short wild bivouacs with views over the Bay of Tunis.

name: Jebel Boukornine
description: Twin-peak forested mountain within Boukornine National Park south of Tunis. Forest camping in pine and Aleppo pine zones on the outer park perimeter.

name: Ghabet El Bondek – Beni Khiar
description: Dense pine forest in Beni Khiar near Nabeul city. One of Cap Bon's best-loved wild camping forests with shaded clearings.

name: La Crique de Robinson – Nabel
description: Hidden cove south of Nabeul city with crystal-clear turquoise water. Very popular summer wild camping spot among Tunisian youth groups.

name: Port aux Princes – Takelsa
description: Long sandy beach on the Gulf of Hammamet coastline. Campers line the beach with tents directly on the sand under pine trees.

name: El Rtiba – Nabel
description: Scenic coastal area near Nabeul with pine forests meeting the sea. Wild camping in the forested dunes above the shoreline.

name: Cap Bon – El Haouaria
description: Rugged northern tip of Cap Bon peninsula with dramatic cliffs and sea views. Wild camping near ancient cave quarries and falconry hunting grounds.

name: Plage de Sidi Daoud
description: Quiet fishing village beach at the northern tip of Cap Bon. Sandy beach with clear waters and minimal tourist development; good for wild camping.

name: Forêt de Beni Khiar
description: Pine and eucalyptus forest between Beni Khiar and the sea. Shaded wild camping clearings used by local campers throughout summer.

name: Oued El Kebir – Cap Bon
description: River valley cutting through Cap Bon's forested interior. Riparian camping with freshwater access and shade in an otherwise arid peninsula.

name: Jebel Abiodh – Cap Bon
description: Limestone ridge forming the spine of Cap Bon. Panoramic views over both gulfs of Tunis and Hammamet. Sparse scrub vegetation with open summit camping.

name: Kélibia – Plage Nord
description: Long sandy beach north of Kélibia town backed by dunes. Wild camping below the medieval fort headland with sea breezes.

name: Plage de Menzel Temime
description: Undeveloped coastal strip near Menzel Temime with shallow sandy beaches. Pines meet the shoreline providing natural shade for campers.

name: Zembra Island (Ile Zembra)
description: Protected island nature reserve in the Gulf of Tunis. Wild camping is practiced by those arriving by private boat; spectacular marine biodiversity.

name: Jebel Serj – Versant Est
description: Eastern flank of the saddle-shaped Jebel Serj with Aleppo pine and cedar. Home to the longest cave system in Tunisia; overnight camping among pines.

name: Bargou Mountain – Jebel Bargou
description: Known as Tunisia's most beautiful mountain at 1266 m. Stunning plateaus and ridges accessible by car from Bargou town; wild camping on summit plateau.

name: Barrage Sidi Salem – Rive Est
description: One of Tunisia's largest reservoirs. Eastern shore flanked by oak forest and olive groves; wild camping on the shoreline in wooded clearings.

name: Jebel Serdj – Zone Sud
description: Southern limestone plateau of Jebel Serdj NP with sparse juniper and pine. Quiet overnight spots away from trails.

name: Ain Tebournok Forest
description: Interior forest between Siliana and Makthar with varied oak and wild olive cover. Remote camping amid interior Tunisia's rolling hills.

name: Jugurtha's Table (Kef Jugurtha)
description: Spectacular 80-hectare flat-topped mesa at 1271 m with vertical cliffs. Named after Numidian king Jugurtha. Camping on the plateau is practiced by hikers.

name: Jebel Chitane – El Kef
description: Rocky summit near El Kef town with views over the Tunisian Tell. Wild camping on the ridge among garrigue vegetation.

name: Oued Mellègue Valley
description: Deep river gorge cutting through the high plateau near El Kef. Cliff camping and riverside bivouacs in a dramatic canyon landscape.

name: Forêt de Kesra
description: High-altitude Aleppo pine forest near the Berber village of Kesra. Cooler climate with forested escarpment ideal for autumn and spring camping.

name: Jebel Chambi – Versant Nord (Zone Périphérique)
description: Northern periphery of Tunisia's highest peak at 1544 m. Pine forest at mid-altitude with natural clearings for bivouacking outside the strict protected zone.

name: Jebel Chambi – Versant Sud
description: Southern slopes of Jebel Chambi with rocky ridges and Aleppo pine descending into steppe. Open-air camping with panoramic Saharan Atlas views.

name: Forêt de Kasserine
description: Pine forest belt surrounding Kasserine town with natural clearings and streams. Commonly used by local groups for weekend wild camping.

name: Jebel Mghila – Zone Est
description: Eastern slopes of Jebel Mghila National Park on the Kasserine–Sidi Bouzid boundary. Semi-arid mountain with esparto grass and juniper. Quiet camping.

name: Oued Hatab
description: River valley carving through the Kasserine uplands. Seasonal watercourse with rock shelters and shaded spots used for overnight stays.

name: Jebel Semmama
description: Remote summit southeast of Kasserine with mixed scrubland. Panoramic views over the central Tunisian plateau and Atlas foothills.

name: Jebel Mrhila Périphérie
description: Buffer zone around Jebel Mghila NP on the Sidi Bouzid side. Steppe grassland transitioning to mountain shrubs; silent landscape for solo camping.

name: Sebkha de Sidi El Hani
description: Seasonal salt lake attracting flocks of flamingos and waders. Camping on the steppe margins during spring bird migration is practiced by naturalists.

name: Plage de Sidi Bou Ali
description: Quiet sandy beach north of Sousse with minimal development. Duneside wild camping away from resort zones.

name: Plage de Sidi Khelifa
description: Undeveloped sandy bay between Hammamet and Sousse. Open beach camping with access via coastal piste.

name: Barrage El Haouareb
description: Large reservoir on Oued Merguellil south of Kairouan. Steppe banks with scattered tamarisk trees; wild camping on the western shore.

name: Jebel Ousselet
description: Isolated limestone mountain in the central Tunisian steppe with natural flat terraces for camping and sweeping views over the Haouareb plain.

name: Plage de Akouda
description: Northern coastal strip of Akouda with calm shallow waters. Adjacent dune and pine areas used for overnight camping by youth groups.

name: Sebkha Kelbia
description: Seasonal lake north of Kairouan attracting migratory birds. Flat salt margins with open skies; camping on the steppe edge during spring.

name: Forêt de Bou Hedma
description: Acacia forest in Bou Hedma National Park at the pre-Saharan transition. Africa's last native acacia woodland; wild camping near park periphery.

name: Jebel Orbata
description: Massive isolated mountain range rising above the Gafsa plain. Semi-arid slopes with Barbary sheep and wild camping on its lower ridges.

name: Oasis de Tozeur – Palmiers Sauvages
description: Edges of the Tozeur oasis away from town, where date palm groves meet desert. Classic bivouac experience under palm canopy at the Sahara's edge.

name: Chott El Jerid – Rive Nord
description: Edge of the world's largest salt flat outside the Arctic. Wild camping on the flat mineral crust with surreal mirages and halite formations.

name: Chott El Jerid – Zone Centrale
description: Deep interior of the great salt lake (accessible only in dry season by 4x4). Extreme wild camping on a vast white expanse under a starry Saharan sky.

name: Nefta – Corbeille du Paradis
description: Natural bowl of oasis springs below the town of Nefta. Palm-shaded gorge used for quiet desert camping near the Great Erg.

name: Jebel Tembaine
description: Pre-Saharan mountain between Tozeur and Nefta. Rocky escarpment with flat summit camping and desert vistas extending to the Algerian border.

name: Erg Oriental – Dunes de Ksar Ghilane
description: Remote sand sea with towering dunes near the hot spring oasis of Ksar Ghilane. Classic wild Sahara camping under the stars far from any camp.

name: Erg Oriental – Dunes de Douz Nord
description: Northern dune field of the Great Eastern Erg, accessible from Douz (gateway to the Sahara). Open bivouac on towering sand dunes away from organised camps.

name: Erg Oriental – Sabria
description: Remote dune area near Sabria village south of Douz. Dunes reach over 100m high; wild bivouac with complete solitude and exceptional stargazing.

name: Chott El Fejaj
description: Salt lake east of Douz with flamingo colonies in season. Wild camping on the flat sabkha margins in a surreal mineral landscape.

name: Oasis de Kébili
description: Ancient oasis at Kébili with date palms and warm springs. Camping in the palm groves on the desert fringe is a long local tradition.

name: Jebel Dahar – Versant Nord
description: Limestone escarpment forming the northern face of the Jebel Dahar massif. Dramatic canyons and rocky valleys with Berber ruins; remote wild camping.

name: Jebel Dahar – Vallée de Ksar
description: Valley between ancient Berber ksour in the Jebel Dahar. Open stony landscape with sheltered wadis ideal for overnight desert mountain camping.

name: Oued Oum Zessar
description: Scenic wadi in Médenine with ancient Berber tabias (earth dams) and tamarisk trees. Spring and autumn camping in a dramatic valley landscape.

name: Plage de Ras Marmour
description: Isolated sandy headland on the Gulf of Boughrara coast near Zarzis. Wild camping on a quiet beach with crystal-clear shallow water.

name: Île de Djerba – Plage de Sidi Garrous
description: Northern coast of Djerba with remote sandy beach and dunes. Wild camping away from resort zones with access via dirt track through olive groves.

name: Île de Djerba – Plage de Guellala
description: Southern Djerba beach near the artisan village of Guellala. Shallow lagoon waters and isolated beach suitable for wild camping.

name: Île de Djerba – Spiaggia Naturale Est
description: Undeveloped eastern beaches of Djerba below the Roman causeway area. Sandy flats and mangrove-like margins attract campers outside peak season.

name: Plage de Zarzis Sud
description: Southern coastline near Zarzis with long sandy beaches backed by palm and olive trees. Quiet wild camping area away from the resort strip.

name: Ksar Hadada – Wadi Camping
description: Rocky wadi below the ancient ksar at Hadada. Stone shelters and tamarisk shade provide protection; classic Berber landscape camping.

name: Chenini – Plateau et Vallée
description: Ancient Berber hilltop village of Chenini with surrounding rock valley. Wild bivouac on the stony plateau with extraordinary panoramic desert views.

name: Guermessa – Jebel
description: Abandoned troglodyte village on a rocky ridge near Tataouine. Camping among ancient ruins on the limestone crest with desert panoramas.

name: Jebel Sidi Toui – Périphérie
description: Buffer zone around Sidi Toui National Park near the Libya border. Acacia savanna with gazelle and desert hedgehog; wild camping in open steppe.

name: Plage de Bibane
description: Lagoon beach system at Bibane near the Libyan border. Protected shallow waters with vast sandy spits ideal for camping.

name: Douz – Lisière du Grand Erg Est
description: The very edge of the Great Eastern Erg at Douz town. Wild camping on the first dune fields beyond the town with access to pure Saharan solitude.

name: Erg de Nefzaoua
description: Dune fields in the Nefzaoua region southwest of Douz. Less visited than Douz dunes with equal scenic beauty; perfect for totally free bivouac.

name: Plage Aghir – Djerba
description: Eastern coast of Djerba south of the hotels zone with natural dunes and sea access. Camping is practiced in the dune area at off-season times.

name: Forêt de Fernana
description: Cork and green oak forest near Fernana in the Kroumirie foothills. Streams and natural clearings make this a perfect forest bivouac location.

name: Jebel Bir Mchergua
description: Inland mountain of Cap Bon with scrub vegetation and views over the peninsula. Low-traffic area for hiking and overnight camping.

name: Plage de Korbous – Falaises Nord
description: Rocky thermal coast north of the Korbous hot springs village. Dramatic cliff camping above hot springs flowing directly into the sea.

name: Barrage Bir M'Cherga
description: Reservoir in inland Cap Bon with eucalyptus and pine surroundings. Lakeside wild camping among planted forest.

name: Plage d'El Biban
description: Southern Cap Bon coast near El Biban with shallow sandy shore. Locally used for end-of-year camping traditions.

name: Oued Ezzen – Gorges
description: Rocky gorges carved by Oued Ezzen in El Kef governorate. Dramatic limestone canyon with seasonal waterfalls; bivouac on canyon floor or rim.

name: Jebel Sidi Aich – El Kef
description: High ridge dominating El Kef town with Roman and Byzantine remains at the top. Remote sections of the ridge used for overnight camping.

name: Ain Draham – Pistes Forestières
description: Network of forestry tracks north of Ain Draham crossing dense cork oak. Numerous informal wild camping clearings used by hikers.

name: Jebel Mansour – Médenine
description: Limestone massif in southern Tunisia with canyons and palm-filled wadis. Wild camping in the protected rock shelters of the Jebel Dahar.

name: Chott El Gharsa – Rive Sud
description: Southern rim of the Chott El Gharsa salt lake near the Algerian border. Vast flat white mineral landscape for extreme wild camping with 4x4.

name: Forêt d'Aïn Sbaita
description: Pine forest near the Ain Sbaita spring in Kasserine highlands. Spring water and shade create a natural camping spot at altitude.

name: Plage de Bir Zitoun – Sfax
description: Wild olive tree coastline south of Sfax with isolated sandy inlets. Camping on the scrubby dune margins away from industrial zones.

name: Iles Kerkennah – Plages Est
description: Eastern beaches of the flat Kerkennah Islands with shallow crystal waters. Traditional fishing island setting for barefoot wild camping.

name: Iles Kerkennah – Pointe Nord
description: Northern tip of Kerkennah with posidonia seagrass beaches and calm water. Remote sandy point used for tented camping by island visitors.

name: Barrage Sidi Saad
description: Large reservoir on Oued Zeroud southwest of Kairouan. Steppe-edge camping on the reservoir banks surrounded by flat olive-tree landscape.

name: Plage de Rejich
description: Sandy beach north of Mahdia with low dunes and clear sea. Wild camping behind the dune line away from the resort zone.

name: Cap Afrique – Mahdia
description: Southernmost headland of Cap Bon coastline extension near Mahdia. Rocky cape with natural rocky platforms and dramatic waves; wild camping on flat rocks.

name: Plage de Sidi Alouane
description: Undeveloped beach south of Mahdia near the lagoon. Sandy coves with minimal traffic and good access for tenters.

name: Dghoumes National Park – Périphérie
description: Buffer zone of Dghoumes NP near Tozeur. Pre-Saharan acacia and palm landscape; wild camping near the park entrance is practiced by naturalists.

name: Chebika – Montagne et Gorges
description: Mountain oasis of Chebika at the foot of the Atlas escarpment. Dramatic palm gorge with waterfall; camping on the plateau above the oasis.

name: Tamerza – Canyons
description: Largest mountain oasis in Tunisia cut by a seasonal river with cascades. Camping in the canyon valley below the old abandoned Berber village.

name: Midès – Gorge
description: Spectacular deep gorge at Midès, one of the deepest canyons in Tunisia. Camping on the gorge rim with vertical cliff views into the canyon below.

name: Jebel El Asker
description: Rocky plateau mountain near the Algerian border in the Tozeur hinterland. Pre-Saharan wilderness with no facilities; pure wild camping experience.

name: Forêt de Haidra
description: Forest of pines and junipers near Haïdra Roman ruins on the Algerian border. Solitary woodland camping with ancient ruins nearby.

name: Oued Melah – Chott Jerid
description: Seasonal saltwater river draining into the Chott Jerid. Tamarisk and salt flat camping in a surreal pre-Saharan transition zone.

name: Plage de Monastir Nord
description: Sandy beaches north of Monastir near Frina and Skanes with pine-backed dune systems. Wild camping spots exist in forested sectors away from resorts.

name: Plage de Teboulba
description: Quiet fishing village beach south of Monastir with access to isolated sandy coves. Overnight wild camping used by local fishing families.

name: Jebel Zaghouan – Vallon des Sources
description: Hidden valley on the western face of Jebel Zaghouan with natural springs and mixed forest. Shaded and cool; excellent mountain camping site.

name: Parc National de Jebel Serj – Lisière
description: Outer edge of Jebel Serj NP with Montpellier maple and Barbary cedar forest. Wild overnight camping on the accessible lower ridges.

name: Oued Siliana – Gorges
description: Canyon section of Oued Siliana with dramatic limestone cliffs. River valley camping in a remote interior setting.

name: Jebel Kebbouch
description: Green forested summit northeast of Jendouba city. Forest clearings ideal for camping beneath a canopy of oaks and pines.

name: Barrage Beni M'tir – Rive Sud
description: Southern shore of Bni Mtir reservoir surrounded by forest. Quieter shore than the north side; secluded wild camping spots.

name: Plage de Sidi Ali El Mekki
description: Beautiful natural beach west of Bizerte near the isthmus. One of Tunisia's finest wild beaches backed by pine forest with clear turquoise water.

name: Plage de Ras Jebel
description: Coastal strip at Ras Jebel cape east of Bizerte with sandy coves and rocky points. Informal wild camping sites among coastal scrub.

name: Ain Draham – Cascade Hammam
description: Forest waterfall near Ain Draham used as a reference point for local camping groups. Shaded and cool even in summer.

name: Forêt de Tabarka – Pistes NW
description: Northwest forestry tracks beyond Tabarka city cutting through cork oak. Remote camping plots accessed only on foot or by 4x4.

name: Barrage Joumine
description: Reservoir on Oued Joumine with forested hills. Lake edge camping in a quiet valley with good bird watching.

name: Sebkha de Monastir (Sebkha Sidi Khelifa)
description: Coastal lagoon south of Sousse attracting flamingo colonies. Camping on the steppe margin of this seasonal salt lake.

name: Jebel Tiouchcha
description: High limestone plateau in El Kef governorate with alpine steppe vegetation. Little-visited summit with free camping on the broad flat top.
High limestone plateau in El Kef governorate near the Algerian border. Alpine steppe with wide flat camping areas and views toward Algeria.

name: Oued Sarrath Valley
description: Remote river valley in northwest Tunisia with sparse settlement. River bank camping beneath willows and tamarisk in a quiet highland valley.

name: Jebel Nara
description: Mountain near the centre of Tunisia with Aleppo pine and chaparral. Windy summit plateau with open bivouac camping used by scouts and hikers.

name: Sebkha Sidi El Heni
description: Coastal salt lake north of Sfax with flamingo and wader habitat. Camping on the salt flat margins in spring during bird migration.

name: Oued Zigzaou
description: Wadi famous for World War II battles near Mareth. Palm-lined gorge with historical remains and wild camping in the valley.

name: Jebel Nedhour
description: Isolated mountain massif in Gafsa with desert steppe vegetation. Panoramic view toward the Chott basins; camping on rocky ledges.

name: Oasis de Nefzaoua – El Faouar
description: Remote desert oasis town at the edge of the Great Erg. Date palm groves and artesian wells; camping under the palms in complete silence.

name: Plage de Ouled Yaneg – Mahdia
description: Long sandy beach near the El Jem–Mahdia coast with dunes. Wild camping on uncrowded beach sections in spring and autumn.

name: Jebel Semène – Kasserine
description: Secondary ridge south of Kasserine with open meadows and juniper patches. Low visitor footprint; ideal for quiet mountain camping.

name: Plage de Sidi Rais
description: Wild beach south of the Tunis lagoon near the salt marshes. Accessible by coastal piste; flat sandy camping on the south shore of Lac Tunis.

name: Jebel Ammar
description: Small forested hill northeast of Tunis offering quick access wild camping near the capital. Pine-covered slopes with views over the bay.

name: Plage de Sidi Bou Saïd – Cap Carthage
description: Rocky coves north of Sidi Bou Saïd with access via Gammarth corniche. Some small coves allow discreet wild camping.

name: Lac de Tunis – Rive Ouest (Sebkha)
description: Marshland bordering the western shore of Lac de Tunis near Sijoumi. Flamingos and waders; camping on the scrub margins in winter.

name: Jebel Bou Kornine Nord
description: Northern limestone face of Boukornine massif. Maquis and pine scrub with open rocky ledges for overnight camping with Tunis Bay views.

name: Barrage de l'Oued Lebna
description: Small reservoir in inland Cap Bon. Quiet lakeside camping surrounded by fruit orchards and Mediterranean scrub.

name: Plage d'El Mansourah – Kélibia
description: Wide bay beach south of Kélibia fortress with white sand and blue sea. Local youth traditionally camp here in large groups in summer.

name: Plage de Tazarka
description: Sandy beach north of Nabeul with pine-backed dunes and a small cove. Wild camping spots between the dune line and the sea.

name: Barrage Masri – Siliana
description: Reservoir near Makthar plateau with rolling steppe hills around. Quiet wild camping on the stony banks of this interior lake.

name: Jebel Mrhila – Versant NE
description: Northeast face of the Mrhila massif transitioning into the central steppe. Open rocky landscape with juniper patches for high-altitude camping.

name: Ain Khechem Forest
description: Forest and spring area west of Medjez El Bab. Cork oak and wild olive with a natural stream; camping under the trees near the spring.

name: Dunes de Metlaoui
description: Mini-dune field near the phosphate mining area of Metlaoui at the desert's edge. Camping on the sand dunes overlooking the Thelja Gorges.

name: Gorges de Selja (Seldja)
description: Spectacular red sandstone canyon near Metlaoui traversed by the Lézard Rouge heritage train. Wild camping on the canyon floor near the Oued Thelja.

name: Jebel Ben Younes
description: Isolated mesa mountain in the Gafsa depression. Flat-topped summit accessible with 4x4; camping under vast desert skies.

name: Plage de Hammam Lif
description: Coastal strip at Hammam Lif south of Tunis with access to rocky coves east of the town. Small wild camping spots on the rocky coast.

name: Forêt de Ouled Haffouz
description: Sparse acacia and halfa grass steppe forest south of Jebel Mghila. Open landscape camping in the semi-arid interior of Tunisia.

name: Jebel Sidi Abid – Kairouan
description: Low steppe mountain south of Kairouan with flat limestone summit. Panoramic views over the central plains; open bivouac camping.

name: Oued Dekouk – Tataouine
description: Dry riverbed with palm oasis pockets near Dekouk oasis. Camping under wild palms in a remote southern oasis valley.

name: Erg de Tataouine – Sabkha Oum El Kheyal
description: Remote salt pan and mini-erg south of Tataouine city. Stark desert landscape with scattered acacia for windbreak camping.

name: Plage d'El Ketf – Sfax
description: Isolated sandy beach south of Sfax accessible via coastal piste. Wild camping on the flat sandy shore with views toward Kerkennah.

name: Forêt de Haffouz
description: Planted Aleppo pine forest on the slopes above Haffouz town. Popular with local families for weekend camping in the interior.

name: Jebel Lessoud
description: Remote limestone peak in the centre of Tunisia. Very low foot traffic; ideal for experienced wild campers seeking isolation.

name: Plage de Remla – Kerkennah
description: Sandy beach on Gharbi island of Kerkennah archipelago near the ferry port. Flat traditional beach camping on the island's north shore.

name: Jebel Orbata – Versant Nord
description: Northern flank of the Orbata massif with deep gorges. Rocky spires and sheltered wadis used for mountaineering and overnight camps.

name: Barrage de Nebeur
description: Reservoir on Oued Mellegue with maquis-covered hills. Lakeside wild camping in the remote northwest interior.

name: Jebel Souiga
description: Coastal mountain near Bizerte with dense maquis and sea views. Short access from the city for quick wild camping on the scrubby ridge.

name: Plage de Sidi Mechrig
description: Remote beach west of Tabarka between cap serrat and Sidi Mechrig creek. Pristine white sand beach rarely reached without 4x4; ideal for wild camping.

name: Plage de Zouaraa Ouest
description: Long semi-wild beach west of Tabarka at Zouaraa, with adjacent pine plantation. Off-season wild camping in the dune system.

name: Ain El Karma – Jendouba
description: Natural spring in the Kroumirie hills with surrounding cork oak forest. Local groups traditionally camp here near the spring source.

name: Jebel El Fej – Béja
description: Forested summit in the Béja highlands east of Nefza. Oak forest with natural clearings; spring and autumn camping in the hill country.

name: Oued Sejnane – Camping Rivière
description: River camping along Oued Sejnane in the cork oak foothills of Bizerte. Swimming holes and flat sandy banks for tents.

name: Jebel Ghorra
description: High ridge northwest of Béja city with forest cover and panoramic views over the Medjerda valley. Wild camping on the ridge.

name: Plage de Ras El Ma – Nabeul
description: Wild beach near Ras El Ma cape with rocky outcrops and sandy pockets. Accessible by unmaintained track; free wild camping.

name: Plage de Ain Mestir
description: Remote beach in eastern Cap Bon accessed via farm tracks. Sandy and clean; good for quiet overnight stays.

name: Forêt de Kef El Karma
description: Natural forest south of Tajerouine with pines and wild olive. Camping in clearings along the western highland forest corridor.

name: Jebel Bir El Hafey
description: Steppe mountain in central Tunisia with esparto grass and drought-adapted vegetation. Completely remote camping in the high steppe zone.

name: Oasis de Gafsa – Lala
description: Mountain oasis pools near Gafsa town (Ain Biadha). Natural rock pools and palm groves used for camping by hikers.

name: Plage de Frine Nord – Monastir
description: Natural sandy beach north of Monastir between tourist zones with pine tree backing. Wild camping in the pine edge at off-season times.

name: Barrage Sidi Barak – Nefza
description: Large northern dam on the Oued Brak river. Surrounded by pine and cork oak forest; wild camping on the lake margins.

name: Jebel Hamra – Kébili
description: Red sand dune landscape near Kébili with iron oxide coloring the dunes. Wild camping at sunset on the red dunes.

name: Chott El Jerid – Rive Ouest
description: Western edge of Chott Jerid near Nefta with access by 4x4 on the salt crust. Camping at the extreme Saharan frontier near Algeria.

name: Plage de La Galite – Île
description: Remote uninhabited islands 60 km north of Tabarka. Wild camping on the protected island beaches, accessible only by private boat.

name: Oued Melah – Ariana
description: Salt marsh and wetland north of Tunis near Sidi Thabet. Winter camping on the open steppe margins of this flamingo feeding site.

name: Jebel Nahli
description: Small forested hill north of Tunis city near Raoued. Easy access for short wild camping trips close to the capital.

name: Plage de Ras El Ahmar
description: Red-cliff cove beach between Bizerte and Cap Angela. Dramatic red sandstone cliffs with sandy pocket beach; wild camping under the cliffs.

name: Plage de Sidi Gnaoui
description: Quiet beach south of Ras Jebel cape. Sandy cove with tamarisk trees providing shade; free camping on the dune area.

name: Ain Babouch – Jendouba
description: Forest spring area in the Kroumirie foothills between Ain Draham and Tabarka. Verdant setting with running water; classic forest bivouac.

name: Jebel Zaghouan – Crête Sud
description: Southern ridge of Zaghouan mountain with limestone pinnacles and Aleppo pine. Exposed ridge camping with 360-degree views.

name: Oued Sra – Vallée
description: Hidden mountain valley southeast of Zaghouan with seasonal waterfall and fig trees. Camping in the valley floor on natural terraces.

name: Forêt de Ain Defla
description: Natural forest area near Ain Defla spring in the northwest Tell. Dense oak with a reliable spring providing drinking water for campers.

name: Plage des Mouettes – Bizerte
description: Sandy beach near Bizerte lake outlet with shallow lagoon-style waters. Out-of-season free camping on the sandy spit.

name: Jebel Dyr
description: Remote limestone ridge near the Kasserine Pass. Site of World War II battles; now a quiet mountain for wild camping and historical exploration.

name: Bou Hedma – Zone Steppe Périphérique
description: Steppe buffer zone around Bou Hedma NP on the Sfax side. Scattered acacia woodland with gazelle and small antelopes; open-air camping.

name: Plage de Farwa – Zarzis
description: Secluded beach on the Zarzis peninsula south of the hotels area. Olive groves and sand dunes; wild camping accessed by sandy track.

name: Ain Teboursouk – Bulla Regia
description: Forest and valley around the Bulla Regia Roman site. Olive trees and Mediterranean scrub on the site periphery; camping near ancient ruins.

name: Jebel El Ousif
description: Plateau mountain in the Tunisian Atlas near Kalaat Khasba. High-altitude steppe with wild thyme and spectacular desert-facing views.

name: Plage de Nabeul – Secteur Sauvage
description: Unbuilt beach sections north and south of Nabeul city between hotel clusters. Natural dune camping away from the tourist infrastructure.

name: Jebel Sidi Aïssa
description: Sacred mountain in the Kairouan steppe with a saint's tomb on the summit. Open plateau for overnight camping in spring with wildflowers.

name: Oued Zeroud – Gorges
description: Gorge section of Oued Zeroud near its mountain source. Narrow canyon with cliff walls and tamarisk trees; seasonal river camping.

name: Sebkha Ben Ghayadha – Kébili
description: Salt flat east of Kébili with flamingo and desert bird habitat. Camping on the steppe margin of this shallow seasonal lake.

name: Tozeur – Dunes de Sabl d'Or
description: Golden dune field east of Tozeur accessible by dirt track. Small erg with excellent stargazing; wild bivouac far from organised camps.

name: Plage Borj Cedria
description: Sandy beach south of Tunis near Borj Cedria with access to uncrowded sections. Pine forest backs the beach for shaded camping in shoulder seasons.

name: Jebel Skarna
description: High limestone massif east of Sejnane with dense maquis. Remote mountain with very low human traffic; ideal for solitude camping.

name: Plage de Sidi Daoud – Pointe
description: Rocky point and beach at Sidi Daoud fish-canning village on the northern cap. Wild camping on the headland with sea on three sides.

name: Ain El Ghoula – Kasserine
description: Isolated spring forest in the Kasserine uplands with Aleppo pine and wild olive. Rare water source in a dry landscape; traditional camping spot.

name: Forêt de Dkhila – Siliana
description: Planted forest near Siliana city on the Jebel Serj foothills. Shaded and easily accessible for interior Tunisia camping.

name: Jebel Orbata – Col de Sif
description: Mountain pass on the Orbata massif used by Bedouin herders. Camp at the col under stars between two rocky ridges in the Saharan Atlas.

name: Oasis de Tamezret
description: Berber hillside village near Matmata with desert mountain views. Camping on the rocky terraces between Berber houses in a traditional landscape.

name: Barrage Nebhana
description: Reservoir east of Kairouan on Oued Nebhana. Steppe lake with seasonal bird activity; wild camping on the earthen banks.

name: Plage de Ain Tounine
description: Hidden cove near Ain Tounine east of Bizerte with minimal access road. Pristine water and rocky scenery for secluded wild camping.

name: Jebel Chambi – Pic Principal
description: Below-summit approach zone to Tunisia's highest peak at 1544 m. Experienced campers bivouac at the pine line on the approach route.

name: Plage de Ras Ben Sakka Côté Est
description: Eastern side of Africa's northernmost cape with rocky platforms and sandy pockets. Wild camping on the historic latitude point.

name: Oued Tessa – Jendouba
description: River valley south of Jendouba city with riparian forest and gravel banks. Local camping tradition along the tree-lined river.

name: Jebel Hamra – Tataouine
description: Red mountain in the far south Tataouine with iron-rich rock formations. Desert mountain camping in an exotic colour landscape.

name: Plage de Borj el Khadra
description: Area near Borj el Khadra at Tunisia's extreme southwest corner. Erg Wan Kasa dunes and flat Sahara; the most remote bivouac in Tunisia.

name: Jebel Tébaga
description: Long cuesta mountain northwest of Médenine city. Scene of WWII battles; rocky flanks with sheltered wadis for camping.

name: Oasis de Ksar Ghilane – Lisière Est
description: Eastern dune edge of Ksar Ghilane oasis, away from the organised camps. Free bivouac on pure Saharan dunes with hot spring access on foot.

name: Sebkha Oum El Kheyal – Tataouine
description: Large salt flat in deep southern Tunisia accessible by 4x4. Absolutely flat white horizon; extreme desert camping for adventurers.

name: Barrage Leila – Bizerte
description: Small reservoir in the Sejnane valley surrounded by cork oak. Peaceful lakeside camping in a forested northern basin.

name: Plage de Remel – Bizerte
description: Protected beach spit at Remel between Bizerte Lake and the sea. Sandy camping on the tombolo strip with lake on one side and sea on the other.

name: Forêt de Nefza
description: Cork oak and holm oak forest around Nefza town. Dense and quiet forest with clearings; often used by Tabarka campers as an alternative.

name: Jebel Rassas
description: Lead-rich mountain in Cap Bon with old mining heritage and sparse pine. Rocky saddle and ridge camping with Gulf of Hammamet views.
